title: AI chatbot API data exposure
scenario_description: A public AI customer support chatbot connects to an internal API that can retrieve customer personal data. The business is concerned about prompt injection, excessive API permissions, third-party model processing, weak monitoring and possible personal data exposure.

title: Cloud storage misconfiguration affecting client documents
scenario_description: A cloud object storage bucket used by a consultancy team may have been exposed publicly because of a misconfiguration. The bucket stores client security reports, vulnerability scan outputs and project handover documents.

title: Ransomware risk in operational booking system
scenario_description: An operational booking system is business critical and has several outdated servers. Staff reported phishing emails with suspicious attachments. The organisation has basic endpoint protection but no centralised IDS monitoring.
regulatory_context: Business continuity and contractual service obligations

title: Financial API credential stuffing and fraud risk
scenario_description: A payment API receives unusual login attempts and transaction requests. The concern is credential stuffing, payment fraud, weak rate limiting and insufficient API authorisation checks.
regulatory_context: Financial security obligations and UK GDPR

title: University research AI model and dataset integrity risk
scenario_description: A research group is building an AI model from shared datasets. The team is worried about data poisoning, model misuse, weak access control on training data and unclear audit logging for model changes.
regulatory_context: Research ethics, intellectual property and data protection requirements
